entity.summary: Sei-based fully onchain central limit order book decentralized exchange supporting spot trading, perpetuals, liquidity strategies, and hook-based offers. On June 25, 2026, Oxium announced an orderly wind-down because unfavorable market conditions and low revenue made continued operation financially unsustainable. Its front-end interface is scheduled to close on August 1, 2026.
entity.notes: HEI keeps Oxium at limited rather than dead because the announced front-end shutdown date of 2026-08-01 has not yet arrived. Users were instructed to cancel orders, close positions, and withdraw before that date; direct smart-contract recovery is expected to remain possible afterward. death_reason and death_date remain null until the terminal state is verified.

events.title: Oxium announced an August 2026 wind-down
events.description: Oxium announced that it would wind down operations and close its front-end interface on August 1, 2026 after low revenue made continued operation financially unsustainable.
events.notes: This is an announcement event, not a completed shutdown event. HEI should add or update the terminal event only after the August 1 closure is verified.

evidence.title: Oxium wind-down announcement
evidence.notes: Official announcement states that Oxium will wind down, cites financially unsustainable revenue, and sets August 1, 2026 as the interface closure date.

evidence.title: Sei DEX Oxium to Shut Down August 1 as Revenue Hits Critical Lows
evidence.notes: Report embeds the official announcement and documents the August 1 deadline, withdrawal guidance, and financial reason for the closure.

evidence.title: Sei ecological DEX Oxium announces gradual cessation of operations
evidence.notes: Independent report confirms the planned gradual cessation and August 1 front-end closure.

evidence.title: Oxium hook-powered onchain CLOB
evidence.notes: Official site identifies Oxium as a fully onchain central limit order book DEX on Sei.

evidence.title: What is Oxium?
evidence.notes: Official documentation explains Oxium's order-book DEX and hook-based liquidity model.
